_comment: Provenance manifest for official / game-compatible datasets. Updated by scripts/fetch_official_passive_tree.py and scripts/normalize_official_data.py. Coverage is honest about gaps; see docs/research/14-development-implementation-plan.md Phase 0.5.

datasets.coverage_notes: Normalized 5150 passive node ids (numeric + semantic) and 22 ascendancy code/name mappings plus per-class base attributes from the official GGG export.

known_gaps: GGG publishes NO official skill-gem data export. Per the official developer docs, the only official PoE2 data exports are the passive (and atlas) trees.

The .build file format references skill gems by metadata id (e.g. Metadata/Items/Gems/SkillGemEarthquake); these ids only exist inside extracted game files (.datc64) via community tooling, which is out of scope (no bulk extraction / no fabrication).
Skill gem_id values therefore stay TBD in build cards and the .build exporter refuses to emit until a verified source is ingested.
No official GGG support-gem export exists (same constraint as skill-gems).
The .build format references supports by metadata id (e.g. Metadata/Items/Gems/SupportGemAftershock); not available from any official export.
No official GGG base-item export exists.

Note: the official .build inventory_slots use a slot id (e.g. Weapon1) plus free-text stat-priority hints, NOT a base-item id, so .build export does not require this dataset. base_item_id stays TBD only for on-page exact base requirements.
